Title: Flavonols improve tomato pollen thermotolerance during germination and tube elongation by maintaining reactive oxygen species homeostasis

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Respiratory burst oxidase homolog (RBOH); plant NADPH oxidase
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ROS are required for polarized pollen tube tip growth, but excess ROS under heat impairs pollen viability, germination, tube elongation, and fertility.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Pollen grains and elongating pollen tubes, especially tube tips
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes heat-induced ROS accumulation that impairs pollen performance; inhibition of RBOH activity reduces ROS and improves are pollen performance under heat.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Two pollen-expressed RBOH genes were identified, with SlRBOHH the more highly expressed one. 2. Heat did not increase RBOH transcript abundance, implying post-transcriptional activation. 3. Pharmacological inhibition of RBOH activity with VAS2870 reduced DCF fluorescence in pollen tubes, especially under 34 °C heat stress. 4. RBOH inhibition increased pollen tube length and germination of are under heat, but shortened VF36 tubes at 28 °C, indicating basal ROS is also needed for normal tip growth.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq expression analysis; pharmacological inhibition with VAS2870; ROS imaging with CM-H2DCFDA; pollen germination and tube length assays
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in tomato pollen; SlRBOHH is more highly expressed than SlRBOHE
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in pollen during germination and tube elongation
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Acute heat stress at 34 °C; VAS2870 inhibitor treatment
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target to modulate ROS homeostasis in pollen under heat stress, but should be tuned rather than fully suppressed because basal RBOH-derived ROS is required for normal pollen tube growth.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: RBOH inhibition reduced VF36 pollen tube length at 28 °C, showing excessive suppression can impair normal tip growth.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence is based on class-level pharmacological inhibition rather than direct gene-specific genetic validation; individual contribution of SlRBOHH versus SlRBOHE was not dissected.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Respiratory burst oxidase homolog (RBOH); plant NADPH oxidase
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ROS support pollen tube growth signaling but excess ROS damages pollen under heat stress.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Pollen grains and elongating pollen tubes
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes to pollen ROS production, but less strongly supported than SlRBOHH.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlRBOHE transcript was detected in tomato pollen by RNA-seq. 2. Its transcript abundance was lower than SlRBOHH. 3. Heat did not increase transcript abundance. 4. Pan-RBOH inhibition reduced heat-induced ROS and improved are pollen performance, implicating pollen-expressed RBOHs including SlRBOHE.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq expression analysis; class-level pharmacological inhibition with VAS2870
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible ROS-homeostasis target, but gene-specific functional testing is needed before deployment.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: As for RBOH class, reducing activity too much may impair normal ROS-dependent tube growth.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not directly genetically validated; included because explicitly identified as pollen-expressed and implicated via pan-RBOH inhibition.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: VF36 wild type; are mutant allele in same species
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter (Pro35S:F3H) in transgenic lines
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core flavonoid pathway leading to flavonols
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: midstream committed flavonoid step upstream of flavonol formation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Dihydroflavonols leading to kaempferol, quercetin, and myricetin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavanones such as naringenin at the 3-position to form dihydroflavonols, enabling downstream flavonol biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies immediate precursors for flavonol antioxidants
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step supported by mutant and overexpression phenotypes; formal rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Flavonols, especially kaempferol (also quercetin and myricetin)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant metabolites that maintain ROS homeostasis and improve pollen viability, germination, tube elongation, tube integrity, stigma penetration, and thermotolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Anthers, pollen grains, pollen tubes, and stigmas
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functional F3H increases flavonol accumulation, suppresses heat-induced ROS accumulation, and improves pollen thermotolerance; loss-of-function are allele reduces flavonols and impairs reproduction.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The are mutant carries a point mutation in F3H and exhibits reduced pollen yield, viability, germination, tube elongation, and stigma penetration, with stronger defects at 34 °C. 2. are accumulates excess naringenin and reduced kaempferol in anthers and pollen, confirming blockage at the F3H step. 3. Genetic complementation with Pro35S:F3H restores flavonol levels and rescues pollen performance and ROS homeostasis in are. 4. Overexpression of F3H in VF36 increases flavonols, prevents temperature-driven ROS increases, and confers pollen thermotolerance. 5. F3H transcript abundance is elevated in are, consistent with intact transcript production and possible loss of flavonol-mediated negative feedback.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; transgenic complementation; overexpression; RT-qPCR; LC-MS flavonol profiling; DPBA flavonol imaging; ROS imaging with DCF and PO1; pollen viability/germination/tube growth assays; in vivo stigma penetration assay; RNA-seq
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Detected in anthers; transcript elevated in are pollen relative to VF36 and VF36-T3 across timepoints and temperatures; transgene increases anther transcript abundance in VF36-T3
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High functional relevance in anthers, pollen grains, and pollen tubes during germination and elongation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Optimal 28 °C versus heat stress 34 °C; exogenous flavonol supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (NCBI SRA: SRP460750); LC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for engineering heat-resilient pollen and reproductive success by elevating flavonol biosynthesis; useful for transgenic overexpression or potentially promoter engineering/editing to boost pollen/anther expression under heat. Stacking with ROS-homeostasis genes may further improve tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No major yield penalty reported here; constitutive expression used, so broader pleiotropic effects outside reproductive tissues remain possible.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study emphasizes flavonol antioxidant function rather than anthocyanins; kaempferol showed especially clear rescue by chemical complementation. The are allele contains a C-to-T point mutation at position 47,450,716 in F3H.
